text:
The Pale Reaches release you reluctantly — the cold clings to your skin for miles after you cross back over the ridge, as if the tundra wants to be remembered. Perhaps it does. Perhaps that is all any of this is.

You descend back through the obsidian ledges, carrying something that was not with you before — not a weapon, not a trinket, but a knowledge. Cold things endure. Warm things burn. And somewhere in the balance of those truths, a door opens.

The mist ahead shifts. Through it, you can make out the silhouette of a chapel — or what was once a chapel — its spire crooked against the ashen sky. The path forward is the same as it always was. Only you have changed.
lore: Those who survived the Pale Reaches returned fundamentally altered — not corrupted, but recalibrated. They understood things that could not be taught, only experienced: that grief without expression becomes armour, and armour becomes prison.
